headline: How Algeria tried to erase the two suicide attacks during Pope Leo's visit
description: Two men blew themselves up on Monday about 40 kilometers from Algiers, without causing any casualties other than themselves. Authorities sought to impose silence on the failed terrorist attack from the official narrative of the historic visit.

headline: Why the Hormuz 'mission' led by France and the UK is largely symbolic
'Kevin Warsh's nomination to the Fed reinforces the impression that an oligarchy runs the US'
French businesses will (mostly) remain closed on Labor Day as government backs down
description: Despite efforts by MPs from President Emmanuel Macron's party to allow local businesses such as bakeries and flower shops to open on May 1, Labor Day will remain a mandatory paid holiday in 2026.

headline: French MPs vote to scrap low-emission zones
description: The Assemblée Nationale on Tuesday chose to repeal a proposal to implement low-emission zones, the Sénat is likely to do the same on Wednesday. Socialist, Green and centrist MPs said they would refer the matter to the Constitutional Council.

headline: Calder at the Fondation Louis Vuitton in Paris, a festive experience
description: The exhibition spans the sculptor's entire body of work and takes over the entire building, spilling out onto the exterior. The journey is a joyful celebration, filled with lightness and movement.

headline: Firing of respected French publisher reveals billionaire Bolloré's tightening grip
description: Olivier Nora, the long-time CEO of the Grasset publishing house, was fired on Tuesday. He was replaced with a close associate of Vincent Bolloré, who took control of France's leading publishing group Hachette Livre in 2023.

Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
description: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

headline: 'The presence of PFAS in my blood is out of my control': Six Europeans on living in 'forever pollution' hotspots
description: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

headline: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
description: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.
